race.name: LatLoppet Madona
race.tagline: Thirty-two kilometers through Latvia's winter capital — a Ski Classics Challenger rising fast in the Vidzeme uplands, where Baltic grit meets Nordic tradition.

vitals.registration: Online registration via Ski Classics platform
vitals.prize_purse: Prize money for Ski Classics Challengers category
vitals.aid_stations: 3 stations with sports drink, water, snacks

climate.description: Madona sits in the Vidzeme uplands, the hilliest region of Latvia, earning its reputation as the 'winter capital.' The continental influence from the east delivers cold, stable winters. Temperatures range from -15°C to -3°C in February-March. Snow cover is generally reliable but can be threatened by Atlantic warming events.
challenges: Atlantic warming events can threaten snow cover in mild winters
Wind exposure on open upland sections
Variable Baltic weather — conditions can change rapidly
Cold continental air from Russia can bring extreme temperatures

course.primary: Rolling terrain through the Vidzeme uplands around Madona. Latvia's hilliest region provides more elevation change than the country's generally flat geography would suggest. The course traverses mixed forest and open farmland, with punchy climbs and fast descents that reward strong technique.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through upland forest and meadow terrain
course.grooming: Good grooming standard, meeting Ski Classics Challengers requirements
features: Set in the Vidzeme uplands — Latvia's hilliest terrain
Madona is known as the 'winter capital of Latvia'
Punchy, rolling terrain with frequent transitions
Growing international field through Ski Classics connection

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A young but ambitious race boosted by its Ski Classics Challengers membership. The series affiliation brings international credibility and a competitive pro field. Latvia's winter capital designation is well-earned. Still building history and field size.

history.summary: Founded in 2018, LatLoppet Madona is Latvia's bid to establish a major Nordic ski marathon. Held in Madona, a town in the Vidzeme uplands region that bills itself as the 'winter capital of Latvia,' the race benefits from the area's reliable snow and relatively hilly terrain. The race gained significant credibility by joining the Ski Classics Challengers series, which brings professional skiers and international attention. Though young, the event has grown quickly, drawing over 1,200 participants and establishing Latvia as a player in the European long-distance skiing calendar.
notable_facts: Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series — the pathway to full Ski Classics events
Madona is known as the 'winter capital of Latvia' for its reliable snow
The Vidzeme uplands are Latvia's hilliest region — unusual for this generally flat country
Latvia has a growing Nordic skiing culture, with strong participation in Baltic championships
Founded in 2018, making it one of the youngest Ski Classics-affiliated events

videos.title: XC Skiing in Madona, Latvia!
videos.description: 10 kms XC skiing track near Smeceres Sils winter sports base in Madona, Latvia. Skis used: Fischer RCR Zero+, footage filmed with GoPro Hero 7 Black.

videos.title: Sacensības Madonas BJSS audzēkņiem Smeceres silā ²

Sveiciens visiem atpakaļ, šeit atskats no ziemas vidus, kad kopā ar Gustavu, Silvestru um Edgaru rīkojām sacensības Madonas BJSS audzēkņiem.

Liels paldies dalībniekiem, treneriem Unai un Airai un organizatoriem!
